extra.reasoning: Both rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b are set true by the same single trigger condition (anticoagulated=true), and no rule ever sets either output false, so priorities/tie-breaks are irrelevant. rec_drug_a is set only by rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50, IF anticoagulated). rec_drug_b is set only by rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50, IF anticoagulated). When anticoagulated=true, both rules fire, making rec_drug_a=true and rec_drug_b=true simultaneously, directly violating the mutual exclusion property (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b). peptic_ulcer and ckd_dialysis only affect rec_drug_c and are irrelevant to this violation. Counterexample: anticoagulated=true, peptic_ulcer=false, ckd_dialysis=false yields rec_drug_a=true and rec_drug_b=true.
